title: aldama squalida
intro: Aldama squalida, also known as the Mexican tea or yerba de la reina, is a shrub-like plant native to Mexico and Central America. It has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant has been used to treat various health issues such as fever, cough, and respiratory problems, and has also been shown to have potential health benefits in reducing stress, improving sleep, and boosting the immune system. In herbal preparations, Aldama squalida is often dried and steeped in hot water to make a tea, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal and health benefits.
